summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones the pinned OpenMeetings commit, injects a targeted regression test, and confirms that attacker-controlled private-message HTML is stored unchanged and rendered into recipient-facing markup without escaping.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should prove that attacker-controlled private-message HTML survives storage and is emitted unescaped by the recipient-facing messages UI, which would allow script execution in the victim browser.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned `https://github.com/apache/openmeetings.git`, checked out commit `41677b22f9ba4236ac890332da823821065a5ca8`, built the reproduction image `evohunt-advisory_01cc35b8-private_message_stored_xss`, executed the targeted Maven validation inside the container, and finished with the stored-XSS verification success marker plus Maven `BUILD SUCCESS`.
verification_method: The helper script runs the targeted `TestStoredXssPrivateMessage` validation inside the container, then verifies that `/target-repo/openmeetings-web/target/surefire-reports/TEST-org.apache.openmeetings.web.security.TestStoredXssPrivateMessage.xml` exists and records one passing test with zero failures, errors, or skips. It also requires the Maven output to end with `BUILD SUCCESS`, distinguishing the vulnerable rendering path from an unrelated build or runtime failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed `[evohunt] SUCCESS: stored XSS validation completed` after the helper verified the targeted surefire report.
The Maven execution ended with `[INFO] BUILD SUCCESS` and a reactor summary showing `Openmeetings Web ... SUCCESS`.
The host-side wrapper completed with `[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, injects the helper test file, and mounts that checkout into the container.
